item.title: Arteta urges Arsenal to 'own' Champions League final
item.description: Arsenal coach Mikel Arteta called on his players to ‘own the moment’ ahead of Saturday's Champions League final clash against holders Paris Saint-Germain....

item.title: PSG more 'hungry' for Champions League after first taste of glory
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain stars Ousmane Dembele and Marquinhos insisted their team are ravenous for European glory ahead of Saturday's Champions League final clash with Arsenal after tasting success last season....

item.title: Trump ends Iran deal meeting: White House official
item.description: The official would not say if Trump had reached a decision on the deal with Tehran during the gathering in the high security White House Situation Room......

item.title: Can Messi deliver again for Argentina at his final World Cup?
item.description:
Lionel Messi will turn 39 during the upcoming World Cup and could have been forgiven for quitting international football after leading Argentina to glory in Qatar in 2022, but he remains his country's talisman as they aim to retain the trophy in North America.
...

item.title: Barcelona sign Gordon from Newcastle
item.description: Barcelona confirmed the signing of Anthony Gordon from Newcastle United on Friday, for a fee that could rise to 80 million euros ($93 million)....

item.title: 'Age doesn't matter' says veteran Curacao boss Advocaat
item.description:
Dick Advocaat believes that he and former Old Firm rival Martin O'Neill are proof of how veteran managers can still be successful regardless of age as he returns to Glasgow's Hampden Park with Curacao for a friendly against Scotland.
...

item.title: Norway's natural-born goalscorer Haaland finally gets global stage
item.description:
Erling Haaland propelled Norway back to the World Cup for the first time in 28 years and spearheads a golden generation ready to thrive on the global stage.
...

item.title: Loyalty could be fatal to Argentina's World Cup title defence: Bertoni
item.description: Argentina are contenders to successfully defend their World Cup crown but their chances will be undermined if coach Lionel Scaloni relies too much on the players who triumphed four years ago, Argentine great Daniel Bertoni told AFP....

item.title: 'Biggest circus in town' the World Cup set for betting frenzy
item.description:
Global betting revenue for the upcoming World Cup is likely to be ‘in excess of $50 billion’, betting expert Darren Small told AFP, for an event he labelled ‘the biggest circus in town.’
...

item.title: Deschamps guards against World Cup over-confidence
item.description: France coach Didier Deschamps warned against being over confident ahead of his side's bid to win a second World Cup in three editions....

item.title: PSG, Arsenal final has no favourite: Luis Enrique
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain coach Luis Enrique insisted his team are not favourites to defend their crown in Saturday's Champions League final clash against Arsenal....

item.title: 124 injured while sacrificing animals in Dhaka, surrounding areas
item.description: At least 124 seasonal butchers were inadvertently injured while slaughtering, preparing, and cutting up sacrificial animals during Eid-ul-Azha celebrations on Thursday....

item.title: 2 killed, 8 injured in collision of 3 motorcycles in Sherpur
item.description: Two youths were killed and eight others were injured in a collision involving three motorcycles in Sherpur, Bogura, on Thursday night....

item.title: Funeral of migrant worker killed in Iraq missile strike held in Munshiganj
item.description: The funeral of Mohammad Shrabon, a Bangladeshi expatriate from Bakultala village in Munshiganj Sadar Upazila and killed in a missile attack in Iraq two months ago, was held in his ancestral home on Friday.......

item.title: People continue to leave Dhaka
item.description: A huge number of people continued to leave Dhaka on the second day of Eid-ul-Azha on Friday....

item.title: Hajj return flight to start Friday night: ministry
item.description: The Hajj return flight of 2026, marking the beginning of the month-long repatriation of Bangladeshi pilgrims after the successful completion of the holy pilgrimage, will begin on Friday night.......

item.title: DSCC, DNCC remove 14,040 tonnes of sacrificial animals’ waste on Friday
item.description: Two city corporations in Dhaka removed 11,029.78 tonnes of waste on the second day of Eid-ul-Azha on Friday, according to officials.......

item.title: 4 killed, 12 injured in Karnaphuli road crash
item.description: At least four people were killed and 12 more were injured in a head-on collision of a bus and leguna at Karnaphuli area in the port city Chattogram on Thursday night....

item.title: Hajj return flights to begin Friday night : ministry
item.description: The Hajj return flights for 2026, marking the beginning of the month-long repatriation of Bangladeshi pilgrims following the successful completion of the holy pilgrimage, will begin on Friday night....

item.title: Ziaur Rahman’s ideals will continue to inspire new generation: president
item.description: President Mohammed Shahabuddin on Friday said that simple and selfless life, strong personality, unquestionable integrity and pragmatic statesmanship of late president Ziaur Rahman would remain eternally alive in the hearts of the people......

item.title: Trump makes ‘final determination’ on Iran deal
item.description: ‘I will be meeting now…to make a final determination,’ Trump said in a lengthy social media post, stressing that Iran must agree never to have nuclear weapons and to open Hormuz......

item.title: PM orders suspension of two DNCC, DSCC officials for negligence in removing sacrificial waste
item.description: Two regional officials of Dhaka North City Corporation and Dhaka South City Corporation were suspended on Friday on the orders of prime minister Tarique Rahman over negligence in removing sacrificial waste in several parts of the capital on Eid day......

item.title: Photo and video journalists in Gaza to receive ‘Golden Pen’ award
item.description: Professional photo and video journalists working in Gaza are to receive an annual press freedom award on Monday for risking their lives to report on the war, an association of publishers has said.......

item.title: UN says protecting children online an ‘urgent priority’
item.description: Making the digital world safe for children is an urgent priority, the United Nations said on Friday, adding that those responsible for online harm must be held to account.......

item.title: Israel, Russia added to UN blacklist on sexual violence in conflicts
item.description: Israeli and Russian security forces have been added to a United Nations blacklist on sexual violence in conflict, according to a report reviewed Thursday by AFP.....

item.title: Iran says no trust in US ‘words’, waiting for Washington to act
item.description: Iran’s top negotiator said on Friday that Tehran would only trust Washington’s actions, not its words, after US vice president JD Vance said progress had been made on a deal to extend a ceasefire and provide a framework for peace talks......

item.title: US secretary of state meets Pakistani FM amid talks on Iran war
item.description: he meeting with Pakistani foreign minister Ishaq Dar — whose country is acting as mediator between the United States and Iran — came days after Rubio’s visit to India......

item.title: Myanmar farmers face fertiliser, fuel dearth
item.description: Under monsoon skies leaden with rain, Myanmar rice farmers despondently tend their paddy fields, doubtful the deluge will bring bountiful harvests during a fuel and fertiliser crisis caused by the Iran war......

item.title: Bobby Hajjaj withdraws remarks on DU following protests
item.description: The withdrawal came after his remarks drew criticism and triggered protests from Dhaka University teachers, including the pro-BNP teachers’ alliance White Pannel......

item.title: Fakhrul urges journalists to remain united to protect their rights
item.description: LGRD and cooperatives minister Mirza Fakhrul Islam Alamgir today called upon journalists to remain united to protect their rights and ensure media freedom......

item.title: Bangladesh prime minister visits Dhaka roads to inspect sacrificial waste removal
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Friday visited different parts of the capital to personally inspect the progress of sacrificial waste removal activities following Eid-ul-Azha......

item.title: West Bengal Muslims crowding border, eager to enter Bangladesh: IAB
item.description: Islami Andolan Bangladesh’s joint secretary general and spokesperson Maulana Gazi Ataur Rahman has said that after the BJP came to power in the Indian state of West Bengal, it has spread extreme religious hatred.......

item.title: Climate change making hajj hotter, less safe: study
item.description: Climate change is gradually shrinking the times of year when the annual Muslim hajj pilgrimage can take place in safe weather conditions, according to a new study published on Friday.......

item.title: Youth accused of raping child in Fatullah dies after being beaten up by locals
item.description: A young man who was beaten and injured by local people on charges of raping a child at Fatulla in Narayanganj district, died on Thursday after being treated at a hospital for six days......

item.title: Derailment halts rail link with Sylhet
item.description: Rail communication between Sylhet division and the rest of the country remained suspended for about three hours after a wagon of an oil tanker partially derailed inside the Lawachara National Park in Sylhet on Friday afternoon......

item.title: US-Iran deal on extending ceasefire awaits Trump’s approval: JD Vance
item.description: United States vice-president JD Vance said Washington and Tehran are close to agreeing a deal to extend their ceasefire in the Middle East war, but the potential breakthrough still hangs on president Donald Trump’s approval......

item.title: Plots afoot against govt, BNP: Rizvi
item.description: Bangladesh Nationalist Party senior joint secretary general Ruhul Kabir Rizvi on Friday alleged that various conspiracies were underway against both the government and the BNP, aimed at destabilising the country, asserting that people will not allow these to succeed.......

item.title: Jeff Bezos’s New Glenn rocket explodes during test
item.description: Blue Origin’s New Glenn rocket unleashed a massive fireball into the sky as it exploded during a test on Thursday, in the latest blow to billionaire Jeff Bezos’s space ambitions......
